competencies.justification: Three architecturally central sections (Distribution of Water and Land, Oceans, Continents) compel students to explain global ocean and landmass phenomena and their spatial distribution — removing this competency dissolves the chapter's fundamental organising purpose.

The 'Oceans and Life' section contains an architecturally distinct passage dedicated to ocean conservation — pollution, overfishing, and the collective responsibility to protect oceans — satisfying the dedicated-section test for Weight 2.

The correlation between ocean availability and livelihoods (fishing, trade, migration, monsoon-dependent agriculture) is developed substantively across three named sections ('Oceans', 'Oceans and disasters', 'Oceans and Life'), satisfying the multi-section continuity test for Weight 2.

The 'Islands' section contains a named sidebar whose primary subject includes India's two major island groups (Andaman & Nicobar, Lakshadweep) and the Indian Antarctica Programme, engaging students with India's topographic diversity.
